nodes.description: Examines key questions being asked about our contemporary food system by examining natural and life sciences scholarship and the applications for public policy.

nodes.title: CREAM 1
nodes.description: The first of a two-course sequence. Students perform the work and make the financial and management decisions associated with the CREAM dairy herd. Prerequisites: ASCI 1000 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Nutrition
nodes.description: The study of standard guidelines to select foods that maximize human health and the functions of the essential nutrients needed to sustain human life. Prerequisites: High school chemistry and biology.
nodes.other_requirements: High school chemistry and biology. HS or College CHEM or BIO;

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission required. Requires a faculty mentor and chair approval. Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. nodes.title: US Food, Social Equity &Dev
nodes.description: Provides an introduction to gender, race, class, and ethnicity with particular emphasis on food, population, economic, and ecological issues in sustainable agriculture, food systems, and community development. The geographical focus emphasizes the United States.

nodes.title: Farm to Table: Food Sys
nodes.description: This course provides an introduction to the contemporary food system, focusing on the interdependence of all components, from farm to table.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum sophomore standing, or instructor permission

nodes.title: Food Systems Seminar II
nodes.description: The second Food Systems seminar; designed to build professional skills and integrate and apply knowledge, skills, and concepts learned through the undergraduate Food Systems degree. An individually-tailored class that enables students to reflect on their learning and experiences as an undergraduate, while also preparing to realize their future professional and personal goals related to food systems. Consists of a series of applied exercises. Prerequisites: Senior standing; 4 credits of internship/research experience from ASCI 1991, ASCI 2300, ASCI 2310, ASCI 2991, ASCI 2995, ASCI 3991, ASCI 3995, CDAE 2991, CDAE 3991, CDAE 2995, CDAE 3995, MMG 1991, MMG 1995, MMG 2991, MMG 2995, MMG 3991, MMG 3995, NFS 2991, NFS 3991, NFS 2995, NFS 3995, PBIO 1991, PBIO 2991, PBIO 2995, PBIO 3991, PBIO 3995, ALE 2991, ALE 2995, ALE 3991, ALE 3995, ALE 4991, or ALE 4995.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing; FS Major Only; Sr Standing Only; 4 credits of internship and/or research experience

nodes.title: Food Systems & Society
nodes.description: Examines key questions being asked about our contemporary food systems by examining social science and humanities scholarship and the applications for public policy.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate students only; Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Issues & Solutions Seminar
nodes.description: Focuses on transdisciplinary research intended to address the 'wicked problems' of contemporary food systems through weekly presentations of on-going research by University of Vermont faculty and Doctoral students.

nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Advisor Permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Human Health in the Food Syst
nodes.description: Explores the multifaceted and evolving intersection of food systems, dietary quality, food availability and human health outcomes. Investigates how political, economic, social and cultural drivers in the food system influence human health outcomes. Prerequisite: NFS 1043 or NFS 1073. Cross-listed with: NFS 2114.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.description: Research requirement (up to 30 research credits) for Food Systems Doctoral students.

nodes.title: Food Systems Seminar I
nodes.description: Explores the Food Systems major and introduces career opportunities in food systems. Covers the field of food systems from diverse perspectives. Students will prepare an academic plan which will identify the course sequence they will take to meet their personal and professional goals and degree requirements.

nodes.title: U.S. Food Policy and Politics
nodes.description: Provides a systems perspective on U.S. food policies and politics across the food system. Focuses on understanding the U.S. food policy process, policymakers, stakeholders, issues, goals and feedbacks between food policy and politics. Prerequisite: NFS 1073 or CDAE 1020 or CDAE 1040. Cross-listed with: NFS 2113.
nodes.other_requirements: Cross-listed with NFS 2113. Prerequisites enforced by the system: NFS 1073 or CDAE 1020 or CDAE 1040. Cross listed with NFS 2113A.

nodes.title: Research Design & Methodology
nodes.description: Deepens student understanding of the various approaches to designing and answering research questions within the transdisciplinary field of food systems.
